text: When you write code, bugs are almost inevitable. The first step is identifying the bug—reproducing the error and understanding when it happens. Don't just guess; use a systematic approach. Start by isolating the problem area, then test one possible cause at a time. Debugging tools like breakpoints in an IDE help you pause execution and inspect variables. Logging is another powerful strategy: by adding print statements or log messages, you can trace the flow of your program and see exactly where things go wrong. With patience and a clear method, you can fix bugs efficiently.
